methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: Validates proposer permissions and block limits
Stores Block in ring buffer at state.blocks[numBlocks % ringBufferSize]
Iterates hookCalls forwarding all ETH balance
Checks TKO balance increased by livenessBond
LibVerifying.verifyBlocks() called if proving not paused
sinks: state.blocks[slot] - Block stored in ring buffer
Hook contracts receive ETH via value transfer
data_flows.trust_changes: User-supplied BlockParams is partially trusted after validation; hooks are called with full contract ETH balance creating trust dependency on hook behavior
validation_points: TKO balance delta == livenessBond after hooks
exposure_risks: All ETH forwarded to hooks - malicious hook could steal proposer ETH
Hook ordering check uses >= (not >) allowing same hook address if it passes the ascending check incorrectly
manipulation_risks: Proposer can choose arbitrary assignedProver address
Proposer controls hookCalls array and data passed to hooks
Multiple proposeBlock calls in same tx share blobhash(0)

path: Checks meta.id in valid range (lastVerifiedBlockId < id < numBlocks)
Loads block from ring buffer, verifies metaHash integrity
_createTransition() finds or creates transition by parentHash
Validates proof tier >= meta.minTier and >= ts.tier
Resolves tier_provider and gets tier config
Handles bond transfers based on proving vs contesting
transformations: Bond calculations: reward = contestBond >> 2 or validityBond >> 2 or validityBond >> 1
data_flows.trust_changes: User-supplied metadata is verified against stored metaHash; transition data verified by external verifier; tier provider is trusted external contract
exposure_risks: External verifier contract is called and trusted completely for proof validity
Tier provider is trusted to return correct tier configurations
manipulation_risks: Top-tier prover can signal RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND via proof data to recover bonds
Contesting with same tier doesn't require proving the alternative - only posts bond
Griefing: repeated contesting to delay verification

path: Iterates sequential blocks checking ring buffer
For each block finds transition matching previous blockHash as parentHash
Syncs chain data to signal service if threshold met
transformations: Sequential blockHash chaining from verified blocks
data_flows.trust_changes: Verified block state root is synced to signal service, becomes trusted cross-chain data
validation_points: Block ID matches ring buffer slot
exposure_risks: State root synced to signal service affects cross-chain security
manipulation_risks: If livenessBond was already returned to assignedProver via RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND, the verifyBlocks still tries to add it to bondToReturn leading to double-payment from contract's TKO balance

path: canDepositEthToL2() validates amount and ring buffer capacity
Encodes deposit in ethDeposits ring buffer
transformations: Deposit fee calculation in processDeposits: min(ethDepositMaxFee, basefee * ethDepositGas)
data_flows.trust_changes: ETH sent to bridge contract, deposit recorded for L2 processing
exposure_risks: Deposit ring buffer overflow could overwrite unprocessed deposits
manipulation_risks: Fee deposit in processDeposits adds to numEthDeposits, consuming ring buffer space
If fee deposit amount is 0, it still occupies a slot

path: Parses proof.data: 4 bytes id + 20 bytes newInstance + 65 bytes signature
ECDSA.recover to get oldInstance from signed hash
_isInstanceValid checks id maps to oldInstance and not expired
sinks: addressRegistered not updated for newInstance in _replaceInstance
data_flows.trust_changes: SGX instance key rotation: old key is trusted via registry, new key becomes active immediately
validation_points: Instance validity (registered, not expired, correct id)
exposure_risks: Side-channel attack on SGX could expose private key
Instance expiry is 180 days - long window
manipulation_risks: _replaceInstance does not set addressRegistered[newInstance] = true, allowing the same newInstance address to be used across multiple instance IDs
_replaceInstance also does not clear addressRegistered[oldInstance], preventing re-attestation with same key

path: Calls internal Guardians.approve(meta.id, hash) to record approval bit
If minGuardians threshold met, deletes approval and calls TaikoL1.proveBlock()
data_flows.trust_changes: Guardian multisig approval converts to top-tier proof submission; guardians are highly trusted actors
exposure_risks: Guardian key compromise could submit malicious proofs
No validation that _proof.data is valid when forwarded to TaikoL1
manipulation_risks: Guardians can override any lower-tier proof since TIER_GUARDIAN is top tier with contestBond=0
Guardian approval hash only covers meta+tran, not proof data, so proof.data could differ between approving guardians

path: Hashes assignment with blobHash for signature verification
Transfers livenessBond from assignedProver to TaikoL1
Pays prover fee from proposer (ETH or ERC20)
transformations: _getProverFee: linear search for tier fee matching minTier
data_flows.trust_changes: Prover's signed assignment is verified; proposer trusts that the prover will eventually prove the block
exposure_risks: Prover fee paid upfront before proving - proposer has no recourse if prover fails
MAX_GAS_PAYING_PROVER limits gas for ETH payment but could cause prover not to receive ETH if their receive/fallback is expensive
manipulation_risks: If feeToken is a malicious ERC20, safeTransferFrom could have side effects
Proposer sets meta.coinbase - if using ERC20 fee payment, proposer could set coinbase to a different address than msg.sender

core_rules: Blocks must be proposed, proven, and verified sequentially (ring buffer with sequential IDs)
Each block requires a livenessBond from the assigned prover deposited at proposal time
Proofs must meet minimum tier requirements determined pseudo-randomly per block
Higher-tier proofs can override lower-tier proofs
Contested transitions require higher-tier proof resolution
Verification requires sequential parent-child hash chain from last verified block
Cooldown windows must elapse before block verification
Guardian tier is the top tier with zero bonds, acting as ultimate arbiter
economic_incentives: Assigned prover stakes livenessBond (250 TKO) - returned if they prove the block
Provers stake validityBond when submitting proofs
Contesters stake contestBond to challenge proofs
Winner of contest receives loser's bond divided by 4 as reward (75% burned)
If actual prover != assigned prover, assigned prover loses half livenessBond
Prover fee paid upfront by proposer to assigned prover via AssignmentHook
ETH deposit fees compensate proposers for processing L1->L2 deposits
governance_mechanisms: TaikoGovernor with 1-day voting delay and 1-week voting period
0.01% of total supply (100K TKO) proposal threshold
TaikoTimelockController: admin can bypass minimum delay
Guardian set managed by owner with version-based approval invalidation
Minimum 5 guardians with at least ceil(n/2) required to approve

edge_cases.scenario: Top-tier (guardian) proof with RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND signal followed by verification
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Liveness bond returned once to assignedProver, not double-counted at verification
edge_cases.actual_behavior: LibProving transfers livenessBond to assignedProver and sets blk.livenessBond=0. During verification, bondToReturn = ts.validityBond + blk.livenessBond = 0 + 0 = 0 for guardian tier (validityBond=0). This is correct since blk.livenessBond is zeroed.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Minimal - the zeroing of blk.livenessBond prevents double payment

edge_cases.scenario: Ring buffer collision: blockRingBufferSize blocks proposed without verification
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should revert when ring buffer is full
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Protected by check: numBlocks >= lastVerifiedBlockId + blockMaxProposals + 1 reverts with L1_TOO_MANY_BLOCKS. With blockRingBufferSize = blockMaxProposals + 100, ring buffer has margin.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: None - the limit prevents overwriting unverified blocks

edge_cases.scenario: Transition ID overflow in _createTransition (nextTransitionId wraps around uint32)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses unchecked increment of uint32. Comment says 'Not realistic 2**32 different fork choice'. True but theoretically possible over very long timeframes.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Theoretical only - would require 4 billion transitions per block

edge_cases.scenario: processDeposits fee deposit when totalFee is zero
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fee deposit with zero amount should not waste ring buffer space
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Even if totalFee=0, a fee deposit entry is still written to ring buffer and numEthDeposits is incremented. This wastes one slot per block processed.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Minor inefficiency, not exploitable since ring buffer check in canDepositEthToL2 uses < (size-1)

edge_cases.expected_behavior: ContestBond of 1 wei should not affect logic
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In _overrideWithHigherProof when contester==address(0), reward = ts.validityBond >> 1. The contestBond=1 is only used for gas savings and doesn't affect the reward calculation since it's only relevant when contester != address(0).
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: None - the contestBond=1 sentinel is properly handled

edge_cases.scenario: Proving window calculated with lastUnpausedAt after unpause
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Proving window should restart from unpause time to be fair to assigned prover
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _checkProverPermission uses max(ts.timestamp, lastUnpausedAt) + provingWindow*60. This correctly extends the window after an unpause, preventing unfairness.

edge_cases.scenario: SgxVerifier._replaceInstance does not update addressRegistered for newInstance
edge_cases.expected_behavior: New instance address should be registered to prevent reuse across different instance IDs
edge_cases.actual_behavior: addressRegistered[newInstance] is never set to true during replacement. This means a newInstance address could later be used in registerInstance() to get a separate instance ID, bypassing the uniqueness check.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: An SGX enclave could register the same address under multiple instance IDs through key rotation followed by re-attestation, potentially allowing proof replay across different IDs

edge_cases.scenario: Contest bond reward calculation leaves funds stuck in contract
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All bond funds should be distributed or explicitly burned
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When a higher-tier proof resolves a contest: reward is 1/4 of loser's bond, paid to winner. The remaining 3/4 stays in the TaikoL1 contract. When overriding without contest: reward is 1/2 of previous validityBond, with the other 1/2 staying in contract. These retained funds are effectively burned/locked.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not directly exploitable, but TKO tokens accumulate in the contract without a recovery mechanism. The livenessBond check in proposeBlock only checks delta, so accumulated tokens don't interfere.

edge_cases.scenario: ETH deposit ring buffer overflow via fee deposits
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fee deposits should not overwrite unprocessed user deposits
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In processDeposits, the fee deposit is written to slot numEthDeposits % ethDepositRingBufferSize and numEthDeposits is incremented. canDepositEthToL2 checks numEthDeposits - nextEthDepositToProcess < ethDepositRingBufferSize - 1. However fee deposits increment numEthDeposits without the same capacity check, potentially allowing the pending count to reach ethDepositRingBufferSize-1 and blocking user deposits.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Fee deposits are only created during block proposals which are rate-limited; unlikely to cause overflow

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Timelock should enforce minimum delay for all operations
edge_cases.actual_behavior: getMinDelay returns 0 for accounts with TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE. This allows admin to execute operations immediately, bypassing the timelock protection entirely.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If admin role is compromised, attacker can execute governance actions without any delay, negating the purpose of the timelock

edge_cases.scenario: Hook receives all contract ETH balance including other proposers' deposits
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Hook should only receive the proposer's msg.value
edge_cases.actual_behavior: proposeBlock sends address(this).balance to hooks. Since TaikoL1 has receive() that accepts ETH during nonReentrant calls, and depositEtherToL2 forwards to bridge, the balance should normally only contain the proposer's msg.value. But if any ETH is sent to the contract via other mechanisms (e.g., selfdestruct), hooks would receive that too.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Limited - receive() restricts incoming ETH to reentrant context, and depositEtherToL2 forwards to bridge

edge_cases.scenario: Assigned prover submits proof at tier 0 which is invalid
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Tier 0 proofs should not be accepted
edge_cases.actual_behavior: LibProving checks _proof.tier == 0 and reverts with L1_INVALID_TIER. Correctly handled.

edge_cases.scenario: Guardian proof approval hash doesn't include proof.data
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All guardians should agree on exact same proof data
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In GuardianProver.approve(), hash = keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)). The _proof parameter is not included in the hash. The last guardian to trigger the threshold determines the actual proof.data passed to TaikoL1. If proof.data contains RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND magic value, one guardian could trigger liveness bond return that others didn't approve.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A malicious guardian who triggers the threshold could pass different proof.data than what other guardians intended, specifically to trigger RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple blocks proposed in same L1 transaction share same blobhash(0)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Each block should have distinct blob data
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If proposeBlock is called multiple times in same transaction (e.g., via multicall), they share the same blob. This is documented but could lead to identical L2 blocks.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Proposer could deliberately create duplicate L2 blocks sharing same blob data

assumption_violations: TKO token is assumed to not have fee-on-transfer behavior - the livenessBond balance check in proposeBlock would fail if fees are taken
External verifier contracts are assumed to be correctly implemented and non-malicious
Tier provider is assumed to return consistent and valid tier configurations
SignalService is assumed to correctly implement cross-chain data syncing
ECDSA.recover is assumed to always return address(0) for invalid signatures (OpenZeppelin implementation does revert on some malformed inputs)
block.prevrandao is assumed to provide sufficient randomness for tier selection - validators could potentially influence this
EIP-4844 blobhash opcode is assumed to be available on deployment chain
game_theory_risks: Assigned prover can intentionally not prove, losing livenessBond but potentially profiting if prover fee > livenessBond/2 (half goes to actual prover)
Contesting is relatively cheap (loss only 1/4 of contestBond) compared to potential gains, encouraging speculative contesting
Guardian provers have ultimate power with zero bonds - collusion among minGuardians can override any proof
Proposers can grief provers by proposing blocks with manipulated parameters that make proving difficult/impossible
Block proposer controls coinbase parameter which receives ERC20 prover fees - mismatch between msg.sender and meta.coinbase in fee routing
integration_risks: Dependency on external tier_provider contract for tier configurations - if misconfigured, could break proving
Dependency on external verifier contracts - if verifier is compromised, invalid proofs accepted
SignalService integration for cross-chain state syncing - if compromised, cross-chain bridge security affected
AddressManager is single point of failure for all named address resolutions
UUPS proxy pattern - incorrect upgrade could brick the contract
automata_dcap_attestation dependency for SGX remote attestation - if service unavailable, no new SGX instances
calculation_errors: In _overrideWithHigherProof: when contester==address(0) and !sameTransition, reward = validityBond >> 1 and contestations += 1. But this path means a higher-tier proof is overriding without a formal contest, and the original prover loses half their bond. The new prover's net cost is max(0, validityBond - reward) = validityBond - validityBond/2 = validityBond/2, which is correctly less than full validityBond.

In verifyBlocks: bondToReturn = validityBond + livenessBond. If prover != assignedProver: bondToReturn -= livenessBond >> 1. The assigned prover loses livenessBond/2, not the full amount. The other livenessBond/2 is implicitly burned (stays in contract).

ETH deposit fee calculation: fee = min(ethDepositMaxFee, basefee * ethDepositGas). If basefee is very high, fee is capped at ethDepositMaxFee (0.1 ETH). If deposit amount < fee, fee is capped at deposit amount, potentially leaving zero amount for recipient.

token_semantics.notes: Custom transfer/transferFrom reject transfers to self (address(this)). Uses ERC20Votes and ERC20Snapshot. Total supply 1B. Owner can burn from any address. LibProving and LibVerifying use raw transfer/transferFrom (not SafeERC20) on TKO, relying on TKO being a known, trusted token.
Used for L1->L2 deposits via depositEtherToL2, proposer hook payments, and tip to block.coinbase. LibAddress.sendEther uses ExcessivelySafeCall with gas limit and return data cap for safety.

Used in AssignmentHook for prover fee payment. SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom is used. Arbitrary ERC20 chosen by prover in assignment - could be fee-on-transfer, rebasing, or malicious. No balance-before-after check performed.

invariants.name: sequential_block_verification
invariants.description: Blocks must be verified sequentially with each verified block's transition having parentHash equal to the previous verified block's blockHash

invariants.name: ring_buffer_no_overwrite
invariants.description: numBlocks - lastVerifiedBlockId <= blockMaxProposals ensures ring buffer doesn't overwrite unverified blocks

invariants.name: liveness_bond_conservation
invariants.description: Each proposed block's livenessBond should be transferred in exactly once and returned/distributed exactly once during verification or via RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND

invariants.name: eth_deposit_ordering
invariants.description: nextEthDepositToProcess <= numEthDeposits always holds; deposits are processed in order

invariants.name: transition_tier_monotonic
invariants.description: A transition's tier can only increase or be re-proved at the same tier (with different hash for contesting or top-tier re-proving)

invariants.name: guardian_approval_versioning
invariants.description: Changing guardian set increments version, invalidating all pending approvals
